questionHtml:
In this question, give all answers correct to 2 decimal places.

Raul and Rosy want to buy a new house and they need a loan of 170 000 Australian dollars (AUD) from a bank. The loan is for 30 years and the annual interest rate for the loan is 3.8%, compounded monthly. They will pay the loan in fixed monthly instalments at the end of each month.

Using your answers to parts (a) and (b)(i), calculate how much interest they will have paid in total during the first 10 years.
markschemeHtml:
amount of money paid: 120×792.13 (=95055.60)             (M1)

loan paid off: 170 000-133019.94 =36980.06             (M1)

interest paid: (95055.60-36980.06=) 58075.54  AUD             A1


Note: Allow 58075.60 or 58075.90 from use of some exact values from parts (a) and (b)(i). If their answer to part (b)(i) is greater than 170 000 then award at most (M1)(M1)(A0) for follow through in part (b)(ii).

 

[3 marks]

meta.title: Using your answers to parts (a) and (b)(i), calculate how much interest they will have paid in total during the first 10 years.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 1.4—Financial apps – compound interest, annual depreciation
